🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Ford Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Northumberland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th-14th century
*Coords:* 55.631, -2.091
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ford_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Ford%20Castle/@55.6369,-2.1069,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZOLCr0vPwqnoqhW8IjwSy4jQOdV9tAhvr-TuuVHg_N-5JSgZ58p2TrRhASrBdtYAjKon_5QWYwc5ZKrsS7H-9C9pg3-WCuj2YPvkvq-xZ1NHZZNiW_MUo85Y8f4B7LZ-pSSRIqljSo-aMiL2w=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* The google places and the wikipedia link to seem to be correct but the images we have are all wrong? Why? Clawz please look into this and let me know. If all thats needed is to repull the images we can move this to enriched. 

*Description:*
Ford Castle is a Grade I listed building situated at a shallow crossing point on the River Till, Ford, Northumberland, England.

_Listing 1 of 10_

listing.desc: Ford Castle is a Grade I listed building situated at a shallow crossing point on the River Till, Ford, Northumberland, England.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Brinkburn Priory
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Northumberland
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 55.279, -1.817
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brinkburn_Priory
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Brinkburn%20Priory/@55.2833,-1.7667,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNjQi3UfQlpdDT69vzCD_ugmr3uRr_5rPcXBcQ6V7uSyoO8J0x3axx21OdijF6nYnVETpvKJEbYvuNO_t_Psu-TVVcJ5kxW9zcQtV_n_jK5wE0hMDF2ZfC4g0vLT2bkBJqklvuUUCGa5FXS0w=s4800-w1200

*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/brinkburn-priory/

*Description:*
Brinkburn Priory is a former monastery built, starting in the 12th century, on a bend of the River Coquet, about 4 miles (6 km) east of Rothbury, Northumberland, England.

_Listing 2 of 10_

*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Northumberland
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 14th century
*Coords:* 55.102, -1.869

*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/belsay-hall-castle-and-gardens/

*Description:*
Belsay Castle is a 14th-century medieval castle situated at Belsay, Northumberland, England. It is a Scheduled Monument and a Grade I listed building.

_Listing 3 of 10_
